name: Mooré
notes: STUB: the grapheme inventory of Mooré is NOT yet specified (graphemes/allophones are empty). Mooré (Mossi) is a Gur (Atlantic-Congo) language, the principal language of Burkina Faso, written in the Latin script with Africa-Alphabet letters (ɛ ɩ ʋ ɔ ŋ) and nasal vowels; it is tonal and tone is not marked in ordinary orthography. INPUT CONTRACT: Latin-script Mooré orthography. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode moss1236), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode moss1236.

ancestors.notes: Atlantic-Congo — the classification node this language sits under.
